novel_features.name: Tiered IAM onboarding
novel_features.description: Get from zero to a working billing read-only setup with copy-paste least-privilege policy, a one-click CloudFormation launch-stack, or an admin bootstrap path.
novel_features.rationale: IAM friction is the #1 reason people abandon AWS tools; no incumbent treats least-privilege setup as a first-class, shareable feature.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this first when sharing the tool with someone who has never touched IAM — it produces exactly the permissions this CLI needs and nothing more.
novel_features.group: Onboarding that doesn't make you quit

novel_features.name: Permission-aware diagnosis
novel_features.description: Probes the AWS credential chain and, on an AccessDenied, names the exact missing ce:/ec2: permission and whether you're in a member vs management account.
novel_features.rationale: Translates raw SDK error codes into the one IAM line to add, keyed to the tiers iam-setup emits.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this whenever a command fails with AccessDenied — it tells you precisely what to fix instead of a generic stack trace.
novel_features.group: Onboarding that doesn't make you quit

novel_features.name: Per-account + aggregate org rollup
novel_features.description: Shows each linked account's bill with names resolved, the management rollup, and an inline month-over-month delta per account in one view.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the local cost cache against the Organizations account list; a single CE call can't name accounts or inline deltas.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The fastest answer to 'which account is driving the org bill and is it creeping?'

novel_features.name: Dollar-ranked waste rollup
novel_features.description: One table of every idle, orphaned, or over-provisioned resource the hunters found, sorted by estimated monthly dollars wasted, with a grand-total you could save.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-joins synced resource inventory, CloudWatch utilization, and cost lines — no single API call produces a dollar-ranked waste list.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The table to paste into Slack before the monthly bill lands — it turns 'I think we're wasting money' into a ranked, actionable number.

novel_features.name: gp2 to gp3 modernization savings
novel_features.description: Lists gp2 EBS volumes and computes the exact monthly dollars saved by converting each to gp3.
novel_features.rationale: DescribeVolumes returns volumes; only a local price-delta table turns that into per-volume conversion savings.
novel_features.example: aws-billing-pp-cli waste gp2-gp3 --profile-aws the dev member account
novel_features.why_it_matters: A concrete, low-risk savings target you can act on the same day without touching workloads.

novel_features.name: Data-transfer bleed surface
novel_features.description: Surfaces the cross-AZ, cross-region, and NAT-gateway data-transfer line items ranked by spend, with the AZ/region pair that's bleeding.
novel_features.rationale: Reads the transfer usage-types out of the synced cost cache and attributes them — raw Cost Explorer returns opaque numbers, not the bleed.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Data transfer is the most confusing and most over-looked AWS cost; this names exactly where it's leaking.

novel_features.name: Deterministic bill Q&A router
novel_features.description: Answers known questions about your bill (top services, biggest month-over-month mover, per-account total, transfer spend) straight from the local cache; emits the relevant slice as JSON for anything it can't answer directly.
novel_features.rationale: Routes recognized intents to SQLite queries and degrades gracefully to a pipeable JSON slice — works offline, no LLM required for the common cases.
novel_features.example: aws-billing-pp-cli ask "what changed since last month" --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets a non-expert get a plain answer about the bill without learning Cost Explorer's dimension grammar.

narrative.headline: Get a non-expert from zero to a Slack-delivered, plain-English, waste-flagged AWS bill breakdown — and cache it so you don't pay per Cost Explorer call.
narrative.value_prop: A self-contained Go binary (native AWS SDK, no aws CLI needed) that pulls your bill, breaks it down per-account and per-service with month-over-month deltas, hunts real waste (idle instances, orphaned volumes, data-transfer bleed) instead of nagging you to buy Reserved Instances, and posts a smart HTML/PDF report to Slack. The headline feature is the part everyone else skips: a tiered least-privilege IAM setup (copy-paste policy, one-click CloudFormation, or admin bootstrap) so you can actually share it with a colleague.
narrative.auth_narrative: Uses the native AWS credential chain — environment variables, a shared `--profile`, SSO, assume-role, or instance metadata — and signs requests itself (SigV4), so no access keys to paste and no `aws` CLI required. Org-wide cost data lives in the management/payer account; run `iam-setup` to mint exactly the read-only permissions this tool needs, then `doctor` to confirm what's reachable. Resource-level waste scans work in any member account immediately.

quickstart.comment: First: get the least-privilege billing read policy (or a one-click CloudFormation template) to share with whoever holds admin.

quickstart.command: aws-billing-pp-cli doctor --profile-aws the dev member account
quickstart.comment: Confirm credentials resolve and see exactly which permissions are reachable.

quickstart.command: aws-billing-pp-cli sync --months 3 --profile-aws the prod member account
quickstart.comment: Pull cost data once into the local cache so later queries don't re-bill Cost Explorer.
Per-account + aggregate org breakdown with month-over-month deltas.

quickstart.command: aws-billing-pp-cli waste rank --profile-aws the dev member account
quickstart.comment: Dollar-ranked waste rollup for the account — works without management access.
Render the HTML/PDF report and post the smart breakdown to Slack.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `doctor` — it names the exact missing ce: permission and whether you're in a member vs management account; `iam-setup --tier core` emits the policy to add.

troubleshoots.symptom: Cost commands return empty but resource/waste commands work
troubleshoots.fix: You're authenticated to a member account; org-wide Cost Explorer data lives in the management account — point `--profile` at a management-account profile.

troubleshoots.symptom: Every query costs money / bill from the tool itself
troubleshoots.fix: Run `sync` once, then query with `--data-source local` (the default after a sync) so reads hit the SQLite cache instead of the $0.01-per-call Cost Explorer API.
Posting delegates to `slack-pp-cli`; confirm it's installed and `slack-pp-cli doctor` reports credentials valid, then pass `--slack-channel <id>`.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when someone asks what an AWS bill means, why it changed month-over-month, which linked account is driving spend, or where money is being wasted on idle/orphaned resources. It is the right tool for recurring bill reviews, pre-bill cleanup sweeps, and onboarding a non-expert to read their own AWS costs. It is read-only and waste-focused; it does not buy Reserved Instances or modify any AWS resource.

recipes.title: Monday bill-creep check
recipes.explanation: Per-account spend with month-over-month delta, narrowed to just the three fields that answer 'is it creeping?'

recipes.title: Pre-bill waste sweep for a dev account
recipes.command: aws-billing-pp-cli waste rank --profile-aws the dev member account --agent
recipes.explanation: Dollar-ranked idle/orphaned/over-provisioned resources in the member account, ready to act on before the bill lands.

recipes.title: Find the data-transfer bleed
recipes.explanation: Ranks the cross-AZ/region/NAT data-transfer line items so you can see exactly where transfer cost is leaking.

recipes.title: Decode a confusing line item
recipes.explanation: Plain-English decode of an opaque usage-type string.

recipes.title: Auto-report to Slack with attitude
recipes.explanation: Renders the HTML/PDF breakdown, posts it to Slack, with the opt-in Corey-Quinn voice on the human summary.

trigger_phrases: why did my aws bill go up
which account is driving my aws spend
post my aws bill to slack
